hypothesis: If 3D molecule generators are trained with an aromatic-aware (Kekulized) valency scheme and evaluated using GFN2-xTB, then they will produce molecules with higher chemical validity and lower ΔE_relax, Δr, Δθ, and Δφ than models trained on the original GEOM-Drugs with the valency bug and evaluated with MMFF94 because correct aromatic valence constraints and a QM-consistent evaluator eliminate valence miscounts and force-field bias.
